design_system.purpose: Core visual system for public pages, utility pages, and admin dashboard interfaces.
principles: Use a small number of repeatable visual patterns.
Visual hierarchy must be obvious at a glance.
Consistency across pages is more important than local cleverness.
UI should feel stable, readable, and scalable.

rules: Use only spacing tokens for margins, paddings, gaps, and layout separation.
Avoid raw px or rem values in component-level spacing unless tokenized.
Use tighter spacing in admin/data-dense contexts and more generous spacing in public/article contexts.
Use a consistent radius family across cards, inputs, buttons, and overlays.
Do not mix unrelated rounding styles within the same product.
Use shadows sparingly and consistently.
Do not stack multiple heavy shadows in dense interfaces.
Prefer contrast and spacing over excessive elevation.
Always use semantic color roles instead of page-specific one-off colors.
Use semantic colors with restraint.
Do not use semantic colors as decoration.
All interactive elements must use visible focus-visible states.
Focus styling must be consistent across buttons, inputs, tabs, and links.

container-lg.usage: compare pages, ranking pages, utility pages

rules: Every page must use an intentional container size.
Do not let long-form reading content stretch too wide.
Use wider containers only when supporting dense data or grid-heavy interfaces.
grid_rules: Public pages should prefer clean content stacks and simple responsive grids.
Admin pages may use denser multi-column layouts but must collapse predictably.

font_roles.heading: page titles, card titles, section titles

rules: Use a limited and consistent type hierarchy.
Page titles must always be visually stronger than section titles.
Section titles must always be visually stronger than body text.
Muted or secondary text must remain readable.
Do not mix too many font weights in one area.
line_height_rules: Use tighter line-height for UI labels and dense tables.
Use moderate line-height for body text.
Use more generous line-height for article content.

rules: Every screen or section should have one clear primary action.
Do not use multiple competing primary buttons in one section.
Secondary and tertiary buttons must not visually compete with primary.
Danger and warning styles must be used sparingly.
Buttons must have default, hover, active, focus, and disabled states.
Cards are grouping containers, not decoration.
Use consistent card background, border/radius, spacing, and header separation.
Interactive cards may have subtle hover response.
Do not create a different card pattern for every page.
All text inputs, selects, and textareas must share a common visual language.
Input states must include default, focus, invalid, disabled, and read-only where applicable.
Use labels over placeholder-only patterns whenever possible.
Inputs must align consistently in forms and tool panels.
Chips are not primary CTAs.
Selected state must be obvious.
Keep chip text short.
Badges must remain compact and scannable.
Badges must not replace clear text when more explanation is needed.
Use icons to support recognition, not replace clarity.
Important actions should not rely on icon-only controls unless universally understood and context is obvious.
Icon size and alignment must remain consistent in similar components.

principles: Motion should support clarity, not performance theater.
rules: Use subtle state transitions only.
Avoid large decorative motion in admin and utility workflows.
Use motion to clarify hover, expand, loading, and reveal states.
Preserve the same hierarchy and semantic roles in dark mode.
Do not rely on pure black/white extremes unless necessary.
Muted text and border contrast must remain readable.
Interactive states must stay distinguishable in both themes.

responsive_rules: Component behavior must be predictable across breakpoints.
Do not invent page-specific responsive logic if a shared pattern already exists.
Keep tap targets comfortable on touch devices.

rules: Use focus-visible across all interactive components.
Do not rely on color alone for meaning.
Text contrast must remain readable.
Dynamic feedback should be perceivable and understandable.

anti_patterns: Hardcoded spacing values scattered across files
Different button styles for similar actions
Too many semantic colors used at once
Page-specific one-off components with no reuse strategy
implementation_notes_for_ai: Use tokens first, then components, then page-specific refinements.
When uncertain, reuse the existing system instead of inventing new visual patterns.
Always preserve hierarchy, spacing rhythm, and component consistency.
